pairs.question: What is David Brodie's current professional role?
pairs.answer: David Brodie currently serves as the Senior Vice President and General Manager at Citizen Relations, where he heads the firm's office in Vancouver, British Columbia.

pairs.question: Which prime minister did David Brodie work for in the Office of the Rt. Hon.?
pairs.answer: David Brodie worked for the Rt. Hon. Paul Martin, who was the Prime Minister of Canada.

pairs.question: What were David Brodie's responsibilities as the Senior Special Assistant for Northern and Western Canada?
pairs.answer: In this role, David Brodie was responsible for policy and issues management, as well as the operational aspects of leading prime ministerial tours and events throughout Northern and Western Canada.

pairs.question: Can you provide an example of David Brodie's political involvement outside his work with Paul Martin?
pairs.answer: Yes, David Brodie worked as the president of the Alberta Young Liberals. He also supported the candidacy of Premier Christy Clark in the 2011 B.C. Liberal leadership contest and previously worked for Judy Bethel (Liberal MP for Edmonton East) and Nicholas Taylor (former leader of the Alberta Liberal Party and Senator).

pairs.question: What crisis communications projects has David Brodie led?
pairs.answer: David Brodie has led large-scale emergency exercise projects for the Canadian Embassy in Tokyo and the British Embassy in Tokyo. He has also been a commentator on crisis communications for CBC and the Vancouver Sun.

pairs.question: What notable awards has David Brodie's agency or campaigns received?
pairs.answer: In 2013, his agency was named the Canadian PR Agency of the Year by the Homes Report. Additionally, a campaign he developed with Tourism British Columbia was named the best North American Travel and Tourism PR campaign by the Sabre Awards in 2012.

pairs.question: What are some of David Brodie's other professional and community engagements?
pairs.answer: David Brodie is a trustee of the Jack Webster Foundation, a former director of the Board of the Greater Vancouver Food Bank, and a member of the Canadian Ship for World Youth Alumni Association, an international exchange program focused on promoting international cooperation.
